params.time: Free delay time (~10 ms to 2 s), used when Sync is set to Free. Short values give slapback and comb effects; long values give wide echoes.
params.sync: Tempo sync division. Free uses the Time knob; otherwise the delay locks to the host tempo: 1/16, 1/8T, 1/8, 1/8 dotted, 1/4, 1/4 dotted, 1/2, 3/4, 1/1 bar. (On the device this tracks Move's tempo; offline and in the browser it uses 120 BPM.)
params.feedback: How much delayed signal is fed back. Higher values give more repeats; the feedback is filtered and saturated, but high settings still create long tails.
params.tone: Color of the repeats. Low = dark/dub (each echo gets thinner and warmer); high = open and bright.
params.mod: Depth of a slow LFO that wobbles the delay time, adding tape/analog chorus and movement to the tail.
params.width: Stereo character. Low keeps channels independent; centre is natural stereo; high becomes a ping-pong as feedback crosses between left and right.
params.drive: Tape-style saturation in the feedback path. Higher values warm and compress the repeats, good for driven dub sustains.
params.space: Blends a reverb tail into the wet signal. Low = pure delay; high = the repeats smear into a lush reverberant wash.
params.mix: Wet/dry balance. Low keeps the original signal forward for insert use; high emphasizes the delayed/reverberant signal.
